warnings.message:
URDF compile warning (non-blocking): geometry overlap check reported overlaps.
Geometry overlap check failed: part AABBs intersect at sampled articulation poses. worst_pair=('left_handlebar'[1], 'left_pedal'[2]) min_overlap_depth=0.008949m overlap_volume=1.524e-05m^3 pose_index=29 pose=(crank_spin=1.571, fan_spin=3.142, left_handlebar_swing=0.44, left_pedal_spin=3.142, right_handlebar_swing=0.44, right_pedal_spin=3.142, seat_mount=0). elem_a=(name='left_handlebar__left_bar_1' origin_xyz=(0.111, -0.1275, -0.062) origin_rpy=(0.0, 0.9933875949533253, 2.0812846487116725) geom=Cylinder) elem_b=(name='left_pedal__left_pedal_body' origin_xyz=(0.058, 0.0, 0.0) origin_rpy=(0.0, 0.0, 0.0) geom=Box). hint: try moving 'left_pedal' by ~-0.01395m along world x (or adjust the responsible articulation origin/limits / collision origins). Note: this QC is conservative (bounding boxes); concave mesh collisions can yield false positives. Fix by adjusting articulation origins/limits and/or visual/collision origins so parts do not interpenetrate in the rest pose or across the articulated range.
If this overlap is acceptable (e.g., conservative false-positive), explicitly allow the specific pair in `run_tests()` via `ctx.allow_overlap(link_a, link_b, reason=...)` and then run `ctx.warn_if_overlaps(...)` so the allowance is tracked; otherwise adjust geometry/joints.
